_note: Sprint D4 Phase C.8 example — UK 3-bed dwelling with EXPLICIT ring continuity engineering. PRIMARY purpose: exercise the new circuits[].ring_endpoints block (v2.0) AND demonstrate INV-14 (ring final circuit continuity) PASSING cleanly on both ring final circuits. Both rings (GF + 1F) are correctly engineered with endpoint_a and endpoint_b landing at the SAME mcb_way_id with continuity_verified=true. Secondary purpose: one FCU spur on the GF ring (kitchen under-cabinet hood) exercises INV-17 (AMD 2 FCU spur modelling) PASS — 13 A FCU × 230 V = 2990 W limit; 1800 W downstream gives 40 % headroom. This is the PASS counterpart to uk-3bed-with-ring-continuity-VIOLATION (C.9) which fires INV-14 FAIL HIGH. No Part-7 rooms are present (no bathroom_zone / pool / sauna / medical / external_landscape) so consumed_intents.special_locations_zoning is intentionally omitted — the cascade trigger does not fire and the cascade allOf clause does not require population.
site_brief: UK 3-bed 2-storey dwelling, ~95 m² total floor area, TN-C-S DNO supply per BS 7671:2018+A2:2022. Consumer unit CU-MAIN is a 12-way Amendment-3 board with 6 kA Icu busbar. Two ring final circuits land at the same MCB way on each ring (CU-MAIN-WAY-1 for GF; CU-MAIN-WAY-2 for 1F) with continuity verified at first fix and end-to-end resistance measurements per IET On-Site Guide Appendix H. One dedicated radial feeds the cooker outlet at the kitchen (32 A on 6 mm² T+E). The GF ring carries one 13 A fused connection unit (FCU) spur for a kitchen under-cabinet appliance (extractor hood, 1800 W downstream). All sockets are BS 1363 13 A double switched general-purpose outlets unless noted. No bathroom shaver supply or outdoor socket on this design — the dwelling intentionally avoids Part-7 special-location rooms so the example focuses cleanly on the ring continuity engineering.

_fcu_spur_intent._purpose: kitchen under-cabinet extractor hood, fixed connection via 13 A switched FCU
